spec_match.rationale: Each row now applies anchor-scope: --tag, so every reused --tag anchor is private to its row while the disabled state exposes the duplicate-name collision.

interactive.rationale: The scope dropdown switches between row scope and none, and the live readout reports the measured badge-to-row attachment count.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The concept page and feature index both describe row-level scope and the final-tag collision in the disabled state.

edge_cases.rationale: The unscoped mode is visibly worse: five badges stack on the final List B tag, and the scoped mode measures 6/6 badges resolving to their own row.

design_system.rationale: CSS variables, --accent-rose/--bg-ivory badge passes AA, no static-card pattern.

summary: Verified with chrome-devtools-mcp in Chrome/148.0.0.0 at http://localhost:3000/v131/css-anchor-positioning-anchor-scope/list-collision/. Before the fix, both dropdown modes measured 6/6 badges attached to their own row, so the page did not demonstrate a collision. After the fix, the row-scope mode measures 6/6 attached with computed anchor-scope: --tag, while none (collide) measures 1/6 attached and five badges stacked on the final List B / brand refresh tag. Console had no warnings/errors and network requests succeeded.
